items.definition_en: The baseline is the line that marks the back of a tennis court.
items.example_en: He stood near the baseline waiting for the serve.

items.definition_en: A forehand is a tennis stroke made with the palm facing forward.
items.example_en: She hit a strong forehand down the line.

items.definition_en: A backhand is a stroke made with the back of the hand facing the direction of the stroke.
items.example_en: His backhand has improved a lot this season.

items.definition_en: A rally is a long series of back-and-forth shots during a tennis game.
items.example_en: The rally lasted for more than twenty shots.

items.definition_en: An ace is a serve that the opponent cannot touch.
items.example_en: She won the point with an ace.

items.definition_en: A volley is a shot made before the ball bounces on the court.
items.example_en: He rushed to the net and played a quick volley.

items.definition_en: A match point is a point that could win the match for one player.
items.example_en: She faced a match point in the final set.

items.definition_en: A grand slam is one of the four major tennis tournaments.
items.example_en: He dreams of winning a grand slam someday.

items.definition_en: A double fault is when a player fails both serve attempts.
items.example_en: She lost the game due to a double fault.

items.definition_en: A tie break is a special game played to decide a set when the score is even.
items.example_en: The set ended in a dramatic tie break.

items.definition_en: A court is the area where a tennis match is played.
items.example_en: The court was slippery after the rain.

items.definition_en: A seed is a player who is given a ranking in a tournament.
items.example_en: He is the top seed in this tournament.

items.definition_en: A draw is the schedule that shows who plays against whom in a tournament.
items.example_en: The tournament draw was announced yesterday.

items.definition_en: A clay court is a type of tennis court with a surface made of crushed brick.
items.example_en: Playing on a clay court requires different tactics.

items.definition_en: A service box is the area where the serve must land in tennis.
items.example_en: She aimed her serve into the service box.

items.definition_en: A wild card is a special entry into a tournament given to a player.
items.example_en: She received a wild card for the main draw.

items.definition_en: A qualifier is a player who advances to the main event through a preliminary round.
items.example_en: The qualifier caused an upset in the first round.
